ShortDescription.Value: In-game frame health, sync-point and memory monitor — find which mod stresses your main thread.

Publish.LongDescription:
## Vanilla Profiler
Diagnostic mod for Cities: Skylines II. Two audiences in one install — a traffic-light health overlay for normal players, and a main-thread / sync-point / memory analyzer for mod authors and power users.
### For players
- **Status screen** — Good / Warning / Problem with the likely cause and one-line advice - **Diagnosis screen** — plain-language explanation when something is wrong - **Memory leak detector** — flags sustained managed-heap growth - **Bottleneck verdict** — RenderBound / SimBound / MemoryBound / Balanced with concrete next step - **Auto-screenshots on stutters** — frames over 100 ms get captured to disk - **Ctrl+F11 support file** — one-click bundle (system info, mods, stats, log tail) to send to mod authors
### For mod authors and power users
- **Per-system main-thread cost** — Stopwatch around every SystemBase.Update separates vanilla vs each mod; assemblies attributed by ModAttribution - **Sync-point flagging** — Update() calls above the threshold (default 0.5 ms, tunable) tagged "[likely sync point]" in the log; per-system suspect-call counter - **ECB.Playback timing** — Harmony hook on EntityCommandBuffer.Playback surfaces structural-change cost separately from system Update time - **GPU/CPU thread breakdown** — Unity ProfilerRecorder for CPU Main / CPU Render / GPU Frame Time (real hardware numbers, not driver-reported) - **Full 5-second PERF reports** in VanillaProfiler.log — phase tables, top vanilla and mod systems by main-thread time, ECB cost, memory deltas - **Memory metrics** — managed (GC), Mono heap, native alloc/reserved, GPU driver memory, growth rate per second, baseline delta - **Harmony conflict detector** — lists methods patched by more than one mod
### Scope of measurement (read this)
Per-system numbers reflect **main-thread cost only** — scheduling overhead, sync points, structural changes, ECB playback, and synchronous main-thread work. Job execution on worker threads is **not** captured: Burst-compiled jobs run as native code outside SystemBase.Update() and cannot be instrumented from a mod. For per-job analysis attach **Unity Profiler** to the running game.
Frame time, GPU frame time, CPU main/render thread time, all memory metrics, frame spike detection and sync-point flagging are accurate.
### Hotkeys
- **Ctrl+F7** — Toggle spike screenshots - **Ctrl+F8** — Settings panel - **Ctrl+F9** — Cycle overlay mode (Status → Diagnosis → Details → Hidden) - **Ctrl+F10** — Force log dump - **Ctrl+F11** — Create a support file (system info, mods, stats, log tail) - **Ctrl+F12** — Cycle overlay position
### Output locations
- Continuous log: `Logs/VanillaProfiler.log` - Diagnostic reports: `Reports/CSII_Report_*.txt` - Spike screenshots: `Logs/spikes/spike_*.png` - Settings: `VanillaProfiler/settings.json`
All paths are inside `%LOCALAPPDATA%Low\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\`.
### Compatibility
- **Works with any mod combination** — no gameplay changes, no entity creation, no save persistence - **Read-only ECS queries** — never mutates game state - **Detects Harmony patch conflicts** — lists methods patched by more than one mod
### Performance overhead
Adds approximately **15 microseconds per frame** from Stopwatch instrumentation across ~300 systems. Negligible compared to the systems being measured.
### How to share a bug report
1. Press **Ctrl+F11** while the lag is happening 2. Find `CSII_Report_*.txt` in your Reports folder 3. Attach it to your bug report — it includes system info, loaded mods, the latest report window, top mods, and the last 50 lines of the perf log
Publish.ChangeLog: v1.4.0 — sync-point detection and accurate measurement scope - Per-system numbers now labelled as main-thread cost: scheduling overhead, sync points, structural changes, ECB playback. Burst-compiled jobs running on worker threads are not captured — for per-job analysis use Unity Profiler. - New: sync-point detection. Per-system Update() calls above SyncPointThresholdMs (default 0.5 ms) are flagged as "[likely sync point]" in the log — these are the real main-thread bottlenecks worth investigating. - New: ECB.Playback timing. EntityCommandBuffer playback is always main-thread and is now timed separately under Phases. - New: aggregate worker-thread metrics. When the build exposes the markers, "Job workers" line shows total job execution time and main-thread wait-on-workers per frame (no per-system attribution). - Renamed report fields: "Top Systems" / "System Time" → "Top Main-Thread Systems" / "Main-Thread Cost" to match what is actually measured. - Removed GC garbage from MemorySampler and report-builder hot paths (pooled sort buffers, reused ProfilerRecorderSample buffer). - Log file is now truncated at every game start instead of growing indefinitely — keeps VanillaProfiler.log focused on the current session, never balloons across runs.
